Haza sang in many languages and mixed traditional Middle Eastern music with modern pop. She became known as the Madonna of the East.
activity_question: What nickname was Ofra Haza given?
bonus_fact: Ofra Haza performed at the 1994 Nobel Peace Prize ceremony in Oslo, Norway.

title: A Star is Born in Arizona
story: Lights, camera, action! Erika Alexander burst onto the scene in 1969, born in Winslow, Arizona on November 19. This future star would go on to dazzle audiences in hit shows like The Cosby Show and later in American Fiction. Her journey from a small Arizona town to the bright lights of Hollywood is a tale of talent and determination.
activity_question: In which state was Erika Alexander born?

title: T.C. Carson: From Chicago to TV Stardom
story: Terrence Carson, better known as T.C., first opened his eyes in Chicago on November 19, 1958. This talented actor would grow up to charm audiences on the hit TV show Living Single. T.C.s journey from the Windy City to the small screen shows how following your passion can lead to big dreams coming true.

title: Mystery Writer's Secret Identity Revealed
story: A master of mystery was born on November 19, 1973. Known by the pen name Ryukishi07, this Japanese writer keeps fans guessing both on and off the page. Born in Chiba Prefecture, Ryukishi07 created the popular When They Cry series. The author's true identity remains a secret, adding an extra layer of intrigue to their thrilling stories.

title: A Warrior Queen's Birth
story: Swords clashed and horses thundered as a brave queen led her army into battle. This wasn't just any ruler, but Lakshmi Bai, the fierce Queen of Jhansi. Born on November 19, 1835, in Benares, India, she would grow up to become a legendary leader. Lakshmi Bai fought bravely in the Indian Mutiny of 1857-58, defending her kingdom against powerful forces.
activity_question: What was Lakshmi Bai's title?

title: Crystallizing Enzymes: A Nobel Discovery
story: Enzymes are tiny helpers in our bodies, but can you imagine seeing one? James B. Sumner could! Born in Canton, Massachusetts in 1887 on November 19, this American chemist made a groundbreaking discovery. He found a way to turn enzymes into crystals, letting scientists study them up close. His work was so important, he won the Nobel Prize in 1946.
activity_question: What did James B. Sumner discover about enzymes?
activity_choices: That enzymes are made of sugar
That enzymes glow in the dark
follow_up_question: Why do you think being able to see enzymes as crystals might be helpful for scientists?

title: Stargazer's Cosmic Prediction
story: Hendrik Van de Hulst peered into the cosmos and made a stellar discovery. Born in Utrecht, Netherlands in 1918, this Dutch astronomer correctly predicted the existence of a special signal from hydrogen in space. His insight about the 21 cm line helped scientists map our galaxy in new ways on November 19 and beyond.
activity_question: What did Hendrik Van de Hulst predict?
activity_choices: The existence of a special signal from hydrogen in space
The birth of a new star
activity_answer: The existence of a special signal from hydrogen in space
follow_up_question: How do you think astronomers use signals from space to learn about our galaxy?

title: Cracking the Mayan Code
story: Yuri Knorozov, born in 1922, unlocked ancient secrets! This Russian linguist figured out how to read Mayan writing that stumped experts for centuries. Growing up in Ukraine, Knorozov became fascinated by mysterious symbols. His clever ideas on November 19 helped scholars understand Mayan history, stories, and culture hidden in their beautiful carvings.

title: Tiny Cell Explorer Born
story: A future cell detective entered the world in 1912. George E. Palade was born on November 19 in Iași, Romania. He grew up to become a brilliant scientist who peered into the tiniest parts of living cells. Palade's keen eyes spotted tiny workers called ribosomes inside cells. This discovery was so important, he won a Nobel Prize in 1974!

title: Magnetic Clues to Earth's Past
story: Rocks hold secrets! Stanley Keith Runcorn, born in 1922, became a rock detective. He studied tiny magnetic particles trapped in ancient stones. These magnetic clues helped prove that continents move over time. Runcorn's clever work on November 19 supported the wild idea that Earth's land masses drift across the planet's surface.
activity_question: What did Runcorn study that provided evidence for moving continents?
follow_up_question: Can you think of other ways scientists might study how Earth has changed over time?

title: A Brilliant Mind in Mathematics and Physics
story: Tatyana Afanasyeva, a brilliant mathematician and physicist, was born in Kyiv, Ukraine on November 19, 1876. She became an expert in statistical mechanics and thermodynamics, making important contributions to these complex fields. Afanasyeva's work bridged Russian and Dutch scientific communities, as she later moved to the Netherlands.
